---
title: "SLAM Metabolic Class -- Figure Deck"
author: "..."
date: "`r format(Sys.time(), '%Y %B %d')`"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    toc_depth: 2
---

<!--
  Scrollable deck of every publication figure, one panel at a time.

  WHAT THIS FILE DOES
    Loops over figures/figure_spec.R and, for each panel, writes it to
    output/panels/<id>.png and displays it with its label. Nothing else --
    no page layout, no grid assembly, no legend stitching. Composition
    happens downstream (Canva); this file is the inventory.

  TO CHANGE WHICH PANELS APPEAR, OR THEIR ORDER OR NUMBERING
    Edit figures/figure_spec.R. Do not edit this file.

  TO CHANGE HOW PANELS LOOK
    Edit ../99_pub_ready_figs/pub_ready_figs.R. Style must be applied before
    the plots are rasterised -- by the time a panel reaches this file it is
    already a PNG and its appearance is fixed.

  OUTPUTS
    output/panels/1A.png, 1B.png, ... S1A.png, ...   flat, one per panel
    output/panel_index.csv                            panel -> source map

    output/panels/ is wiped at the start of every render -- see the chunk
    below. Any panel that is MISSING simply has no PNG.

    Table PNGs are NOT written here. Every table is built and rasterised by
    ../99_pub_ready_figs/pub_ready_figs.R; a table that belongs to a figure is
    referenced as one of that figure's panels and so gets copied out under its
    panel id like any other. Tables listed in TABLES (Table 1, Table 2, the
    supplemental tables) render inline in this document only.
-->

<style>
/* This deck is scanned, not read: the panel id is the thing you are looking
   for, so it is set large, and the image is deliberately small so more panels
   fit on screen at once. Changing these two rules changes the whole deck. */
.panel-id  { font-size: 3em; font-weight: 700; line-height: 1;
             margin: 1.4em 0 0.1em; }
.panel-src { font-size: .75em; color: #666; margin: 0 0 .4em; }
.panel-img { max-width: 75%; height: auto; }
/* forests only -- must follow .panel-img to win at equal specificity */
.panel-img-full { max-width: 100%; }
</style>

```{r setup, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE, echo=FALSE}
# library(helphlme)
library(tidyverse)
library(kableExtra)
# library(survival)
# library(survminer)
library(ggplotify)
# library(sjPlot)
# library(lme4)
library(cowplot)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(viridis)
library(consoler)
library(flextable)
# library(future)
library(magick)
library(gridExtra)
set.seed(365)

# setting global knitr options for r markdown output
knitr::opts_chunk$set(autodep = TRUE)
knitr::opts_chunk$set(warning = FALSE)
knitr::opts_chunk$set(message = FALSE)
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE)
knitr::opts_chunk$set(cache = FALSE)

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
```

```{r spec and helpers}
source("figure_spec.R")

PANEL_DIR <- file.path("output", "panels")
if (!dir.exists(PANEL_DIR)) dir.create(PANEL_DIR, recursive = TRUE)

# Panel ids are POSITIONAL: inserting a figure re-letters everything after it,
# and deleting one leaves the highest ids with no owner. A file written by an
# earlier render is never overwritten in that case, so it would sit in this
# folder looking exactly as authoritative as a current one -- and this folder is
# what gets handed off for layout. Clear it every render so its contents can
# only ever be the panels the current spec produced.
old <- list.files(PANEL_DIR, pattern = "\\.png$", full.names = TRUE)
if (length(old)) {
  invisible(file.remove(old))
  message("cleared ", length(old), " panel PNG(s) from a previous render")
}


# Figure ids: primary -> "1","2",... ; supplemental -> "S1","S2",...
# Derived from position in FIGURES, so deleting or reordering a figure
# renumbers everything automatically.
figure_ids <- local({
  n <- list(primary = 0L, supplemental = 0L, abstract = 0L)
  vapply(FIGURES, function(f) {
    n[[f$part]] <<- n[[f$part]] + 1L
    if (f$part == "abstract") "GA" else paste0(if (f$part == "supplemental") "S" else "", n[[f$part]])
  }, character(1))
})

# Panel id: figure id + letter by position -> "S2C"
panel_id <- function(fig_id, i) if (fig_id == "GA") "GA" else paste0(fig_id, LETTERS[i])

# Most panels render at PANEL_SCALE so more of the deck fits on screen. Forest
# plots are the exception: combined Cox forests and the faceted (quintile /
# tertile) ones are wide, dense, and carry small per-row labels and CIs that
# stop being legible when shrunk, so they render full width. Matching on the
# path is deliberate -- every forest in the deck is written into a directory
# named .../forest/ or .../*_forest/ by stage 07, so a new one is picked up
# automatically rather than needing to be listed here.
PANEL_SCALE   <- 0.75
is_full_width <- function(path) grepl("forest", path, fixed = TRUE)
```

<!--
  This deck deliberately loads NO stage-07 workspaces. Every table it shows is
  produced upstream by ../99_pub_ready_figs/pub_ready_figs.R and referenced as
  a PNG through figure_spec.R, so the deck depends only on files on disk. The
  old `load stage outputs` chunk loaded seven workspaces unconditionally and
  aborted the whole render if any one config had not run yet -- and nothing in
  the document used them.
-->

<!--
  Tables are NOT built here any more. They are produced by
  ../99_pub_ready_figs/pub_ready_figs.R along with every other panel, so that
  all appearance decisions live in one stage. figure_spec.R references them by
  path under ../99_pub_ready_figs/output/tables/.
-->

# Tables

Table 1 and Table 2 -- the class demographics that lead the manuscript.

Numbered separately from the figures, as in the manuscript. Tables marked
`png` in figure_spec.R are also written to `output/tables/` as Canva assets;
the rest render here only.

```{r render tables, results='asis'}
# Split into two passes: primary tables lead the document (they are Table 1
# and Table 2 of the manuscript), supplemental tables trail the figures.
tn <- list(primary = 0L, supplemental = 0L)

render_tables <- function(which_part) {
for (tb in TABLES) {
  if (tb$part != which_part) next
  tn[[tb$part]] <- tn[[tb$part]] + 1L
  label <- paste0(if (tb$part == "supplemental") "Supplemental Table " else "Table ",
                  tn[[tb$part]])

  cat("\n\n## ", label, ". ", tb$title, "\n\n", sep = "")

  out <- tryCatch(tb$build(), error = function(e) e)
  if (inherits(out, "error")) {
    cat("\n*FAILED: ", conditionMessage(out), "*\n\n", sep = "")
    next
  }

  # build() may return a flextable (already themed) or a plain data frame
  if (inherits(out, "flextable")) {
    cat(knitr::knit_child(text = "`r knitr::asis_output(flextable::htmltools_value(out))`",
                          quiet = TRUE), "\n")
  } else {
    print(knitr::kable(out))
    cat("\n")
  }
}
}

render_tables("primary")
```

# Figures

```{r render figures, results='asis'}
missing_panels <- character(0)

for (fi in seq_along(FIGURES)) {
  f      <- FIGURES[[fi]]
  fig_id <- figure_ids[[fi]]

  cat("\n\n## ", fig_id, ". ", f$title, "\n\n", sep = "")

  for (i in seq_along(f$panels)) {
    id   <- panel_id(fig_id, i)
    path <- f$panels[[i]]

    if (!file.exists(path)) {
      missing_panels <- c(missing_panels, paste0(id, "  ", path))
      cat('\n<p class="panel-id">', id, '</p>\n', sep = "")
      cat('<p class="panel-src">MISSING: <code>', path, '</code></p>\n\n', sep = "")
      next
    }

    img <- magick::image_read(path)
    out <- file.path(PANEL_DIR, paste0(id, ".png"))
    magick::image_write(img, out, format = "png")

    # Scaling is applied in BOTH directions. The width attribute scales the
    # image's natural pixel width, which is what shrinks a panel already
    # narrower than the text column. The max-width CSS rule scales it relative
    # to the column instead, which is what shrinks the table PNGs -- those are
    # written at zoom = 10, so a fraction of their natural width still
    # overflows the column and would otherwise look unchanged. Both have to
    # move together, hence one scale driving the width and one matching class.
    full  <- is_full_width(path)
    scale <- if (full) 1 else PANEL_SCALE
    cls   <- if (full) "panel-img panel-img-full" else "panel-img"
    w     <- round(magick::image_info(img)$width[1] * scale)

    cat('\n<p class="panel-id">', id, '</p>\n', sep = "")
    cat('<p class="panel-src"><code>', path, '</code></p>\n', sep = "")
    cat('<img class="', cls, '" src="', out, '" width="', w, '">\n\n', sep = "")
  }
}
```

```{r missing summary, results='asis'}
if (length(missing_panels)) {
  cat("\n\n# Missing panels\n\n")
  cat(paste0("- `", missing_panels, "`"), sep = "\n")
  # Same switch as 99_pub_ready_figs: tolerate gaps while the pipeline is still
  # running, but fail the render for the run that produces the submitted deck,
  # so a missing panel cannot be scrolled past.
  if (nzchar(Sys.getenv("FIGS_STRICT")))
    stop(length(missing_panels), " panel(s) missing; see the list above",
         call. = FALSE)
}
```

# Supplemental Tables

```{r render supplemental tables, results='asis'}
render_tables("supplemental")
```

# Panel index

```{r index}
idx <- do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_along(FIGURES), function(fi) {
  f <- FIGURES[[fi]]
  data.frame(
    panel  = vapply(seq_along(f$panels), function(i) panel_id(figure_ids[[fi]], i), ""),
    figure = paste0(figure_ids[[fi]], ". ", f$title),
    source = unlist(f$panels),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}))
write.csv(idx, file.path("output", "panel_index.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
knitr::kable(idx)
```
